excerpt: Completes customer orders, sources equipment as needed, places service calls, processes order cancellations and requests equipment pick-ups … Working knowledge of Sunbelt's point of sale system, Wynne or AS400 (preferred).
Ensure OSRs and CSRs are aware of slow moving equipment … Various reporting, including, but not limited to: daily contract reports/AP on hold/accrual reports/asset disposal report.

Determine if the inside sales opportunity can be turned into something more — Is this a transactional sale that can be handled by an employee at the counter, or should an outside salesperson respond? … Utilize your database — Most rental companies have a database that includes the name of your customer, his or her business and their purchasing history … Be proactive to determine your customer's needs — Show initiative and ask probing questions. What is the customer seeking today? Is there something else that should be taken into consideration when the order is placed?

Late returns are one of the most common causes of dispatch conflicts in equipment rental. If equipment is scheduled for collection on day five of a booking but the customer requests an extension, the item may no longer be available for a new booking that starts the same day … Managing late returns requires visibility of two things simultaneously: which equipment is coming back late, and which upcoming bookings depend on those specific items.

Sales committing to jobs without full visibility … Availability that looks right but isn't … In strong operations: Sales sees true availability before committing. Dispatch sees upcoming work as it's sold. Changes update instantly for everyone. No one is surprised on delivery day.

Equipment rental software like RentalMan is designed to manage the end-to-end equipment rental lifecycle—from quoting and fleet tracking to maintenance, invoicing, and reporting. It's purpose-built for the unique aspects of equipment rental operations.

If your customer can check out your available fleet on line, generate themselves a quote via their cell phone and confirm it, and that doesn't even need to touch a sales person it can flow directly to your yard manager … Not so long ago, your yard guys needed to take pieces of paper and clip boards into the yard, write equipment numbers on to say that something has been allocated and then they'd hand those back into an administrator to enter into your computer system. This is no longer the way it has to be!
